page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg9i.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, the instance provides dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading, ensuring consistent compute resources for workloads.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPU accelerators, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and lack of hyperthreading overhead provide predictable execution times for compute-bound tasks, though the absence of bundled local storage requires external storage provisioning. This hardware profile is suitable for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and application servers that require dedicated CPU execution and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg9i.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.4 GHz, configured with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, providing a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio with dedicated CPU allocation. The instance includes 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core memory allocation. It does not contain local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network performance is defined by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This instance is designed for workloads requiring predictable CPU performance without hyperthreading overhead, such as application servers, web serving, and general-purpose business applications.
